title: Desolous

'''Desolous''' was a [[Sith Lord]] who was active in [[the galaxy]] long before<ref name="TROS VD" /> the [[Standard year|year]] [[35 ABY]].<ref name="Ep IX date">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'' dates the [[Resistance]]'s discovery of the [[Sith Eternal]] and their [[legion]]s of [[Sith trooper]]s to one [[Standard year|year]] after the [[Hosnian Cataclysm]]. Since the Hosnian Cataclysm is dated to the year [[34 ABY]] by ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'', the discovery of the Sith Eternal must therefore take place in [[35 ABY]].</ref> The [[Sith Eternal]] cult, which was active during that [[time]], had a custom of naming their [[Sith trooper]] detachments after ancient [[Sith]]. The [[Desolous Legion]] was thus named after them—a name whose true significance remained a mystery to those outside of the cult.<ref name="TROS VD" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:DarthDesolousface.png|thumb|right|250px|Darth Desolous in the ''Star Wars'' Legends continuity]]
Desolous was first mentioned in the current ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in ''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'',<ref name="TROS VD" /> a reference book published on [[December 20]], [[2019]] and serving as a companion for the film [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker|''Star Wars'': Episode IX ''The Rise of Skywalker'']].<ref name="Penguin Random House">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/607902/star-wars-the-rise-of-skywalker-the-visual-dictionary-by-pablo-hidalgo|text=Star Wars The Rise of Skywalker The Visual Dictionary}}</ref>

The character originated in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, where he was depicted as a [[Pau'an/Legends|Pau'an]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] named [[Darth Desolous]], and was created as an exclusive character for the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PlayStation 2]], [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PlayStation Portable]] and [[Wikipedia:Wii|Wii]] versions of the [[2008]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'', developed by [[Krome Studios]] and published by [[LucasArts]].<ref name="TFU">{{TFU|version=[[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PSP]], [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PS2]] and [[Wikipedia:Wii|Wii]]}}</ref>
